meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no marketing' barrier. Learn to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to build portfolio-ready apps as a student.
content.answer_block: The 'no marketing' trap is a myth; your code is your best marketing. By using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass months of boilerplate, allowing you to ship high-quality, functional products that demonstrate your engineering prowess to future employers. Focus on utility, not visibility, to build your career.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, paralyzed by the fear that your brilliant code will disappear into the void of the internet. You have the technical talent to build, but the 'marketing' wall feels like an insurmountable mountain. You aren't a salesperson; you're an engineer.

But here is the secret: you don't need a marketing budget. You need a product that speaks for itself. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from 'manual labor' to 'architectural precision.' It’s time to stop worrying about reach and start building the portfolio that makes recruiters come to you.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-grade starter kit to eliminate configuration fatigue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a modern SaaS stack with Next.js, authentication, and Stripe integration using the latest security best practices.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Use AI to document your app's core utility so you can explain it clearly to users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a product manager. Generate a 3-sentence elevator pitch for a SaaS tool that solves [INSERT PROBLEM].
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a landing page copy focused on user pain points'

steps.title: Automate Database Schemas
steps.description: Create a robust data model that supports your core feature without manual SQL overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a user-based subscription service with role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema for user subscription model'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'killer feature' that makes your project a standout portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build an API route that processes user input and returns a high-value data transformation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a server action to process user data with validation'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Get your app live to prove it works in the real world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup production deployment script and environment variables'

pro_tips: Treat your GitHub README as your primary marketing asset; document the 'why' behind your technical decisions.
Ship a 'Minimum Viable Portfolio' (MVP) in 48 hours to prevent feature creep.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your critical path, which shows employers you value code quality.
Prioritize 'Build in Public' by sharing your code snippets on Twitter/LinkedIn, not polished ads.
Focus on solving a specific, boring problem; boring problems have users who actually pay.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, share the repository link in the AI Builder community and request a code review from a senior developer to refine your architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the fear of marketing hold your potential hostage. You have the tools, the stack, and the roadmap to ship something meaningful. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
